Apple is planning to retire its Music Memos app, which was  first released in 2016 as an app designed to allow musicians and songwriters to capture song ideas on the fly. Since its launch, the app  received few updates from Apple, and going forward, the app will receive no more updates.
 According to an  Apple Support document, Music Memos will no longer be available for download after March 1, 2021, but the app will still be available for use and can be downloaded from the  App Store purchase history.
 Still, Apple says that users should export Music Memos recordings to the Voice Memos library to ensure that they're saved, and the company is encouraging people to use Voice Memos over Music memos.
 Apple today updated Music Memos to version 1.0.7, adding a new feature that allows Music Memos recordings to be exported to the Voice Memos library. Apple says that Voice Memos can be used to quickly capture ideas, and recordings can be taken further with GarageBand.
 Exporting Music Memos to Voice Memos requires an  iPhone with  iOS 14 or an  iPad with  iPadOS 14, along with the latest versions of Voice Memos and Music Memos. Exported content will appear in Voice Memos in a folder titled "Music Memos."
 This app had a lot of potential, and it would’ve been great if Apple had kept updating it.
 I'm really sad about this. This app was GREAT for whenever I wanted to take a quick "music memo." It was the perfect Notes version for music when I had a little idea I wanted to record from the guitar without getting too crazy with other apps and having to save things a certain way, etc. Was easy to throw a super basic drum or bass line in automatically generated just to help with the feel slightly. I will miss this dearly. :-(
 I use it at least three times week. I found it much more useful than Voice Memo for capturing ideas as a songwriter. I feel this is a poor decision on Apple's part.
  This app never worked very well and was incredibly sluggish and oftentimes unresponsive. It was a nice idea that didn’t get the attention it deserved. I’m surprised they ever released it in the first place to be honest.

 瑞典要求该国首次向远程学习迈进，要求14至16岁的学生进行远程学习。
  瑞典曾经是没有因不停学或随着冠状病毒传播而要求戴口罩的典型孩子，但随着情况的增加，瑞典将在今年剩余的时间里将所有14至16岁的学生转移到首都进行远程学习。
 “我们必须大幅度减少与我们的联系，所以不幸的是，我被迫建议（斯德哥尔摩地区）市政当局从周一开始为高级阶段的学生提供现场教学，”地区感染控制负责人玛丽亚·罗岑·奥斯特隆德在一份声明中说。报告。 
这一转变标志着瑞典首次涉足远程学习，去年秋天，随着冠状病毒的传播，瑞典迅速成为整个欧洲的常态。瑞典最初吹捧其应对大流行的逆势方法，这种方法主张保持经济开放并依靠公民来确保自己的安全。但是自那以后，由于感染和死亡持续存在，它已恢复为部分限制。
 据路透社报道，首都斯德哥尔摩受到covid-19的打击最为严重，该国7,500多例与冠状病毒相关的死亡中，三分之一遭受了该病的折磨。
 一个著名的无党派监督组织对联邦官员表示关切，称疾病控制和预防中心主任下令销毁具有政治意义的电子邮件。
 疾病预防控制中心主任罗伯特·雷德菲尔德（Robert R.Redfield）周四遭到众议员詹姆斯·E·克莱本（DS.C.）的抨击，后者声称雷德菲尔德试图销毁特朗普政府高级卫生官员不当干预冠状病毒对儿童风险的报告的证据。 。该指控源于一次采访，CDC的《发病率和死亡率周报》的编辑说，她被告知要删除卫生与公共服务部长亚历克斯·阿扎尔（Alex Azar）给高级顾问的一封电子邮件。
 华盛顿的公民责任与道德监督组织向国家档案和记录管理局（NARA）致信，称CDC没有向国家档案管理员报告此事，从而违反了联邦记录法。 CREW要求国家档案馆“采取适当的纠正措施”，包括指示CDC发布有关情况的报告。
 CREW执行董事Noah Bookbinder在一份声明中说：“ NARA现在比以往任何时候都必须提高透明度，并确保代理机构遵守记录保存法。” “美国人的生活从字面上看取决于我们对科学准确信息的获取。”
 雷德菲尔德说，他“指示疾病预防控制中心的工作人员忽略”来自阿扎尔顾问保罗·亚历山大的电子邮件。 
官员周五说，对美国与加拿大和墨西哥交界处所有不必要旅行的前所未有的限制将进入第10个月。
 该措施将至少在1月21日生效，并已于3月中旬实施，并自30天以来逐步延长。
 它们对贸易和基本工人的流动影响有限，但它们损害了旅游业并破坏了边境小城镇的生活。
 国土安全部代理秘书查德·沃尔夫（Chad Wolf）在推文中确认了该措施的扩展。
 他在推特上说：“随着本届政府继续在针对covid的疫苗方面取得重大进展，我们将在新年初重新评估不必要的旅行限制。”
 加拿大总理贾斯汀·特鲁多（Justin Trudeau）本月表示，边境遏制措施将不会放松，“除非该病毒在世界各地得到更大程度的控制，”这表明这些措施将在2021年全面实施。
 美国食品和药物管理局已经授权对消费者进行冠状病毒的首次直接消费者测试，从而使美国人可以自己携带鼻拭子，而无需处方就可以将其送入实验室进行测试。 
尽管医生可以在对患者进行简短问卷调查后开出针对冠状病毒的家庭测试的处方，但FDA周三批准了该法案，取消了LabCorp的Pixel COVID-19测试家庭采集套件所需的步骤。
 “有了FDA授权的第一个非处方居家收集工具包，COVID-19，我们使人们能够了解自己的健康状况并做出自信的决定，” LabCorp Diagnostics首席医学官兼总裁Brian Caveney ，在一份声明中说。 “有了这项授权，我们可以帮助更多的人接受测试，减少病毒的传播并改善我们社区的健康。”
 想要订购测试的18岁以上的人可以在没有医生许可的情况下在线或在商店购买。通过鼻子收集自己的样本后，患者将材料发送到LabCorp进行处理。如果测试结果为阳性或无效，医疗保健提供者将与患者联系。如果测试结果是否定的，患者将通过LabCorp的在线门户网站或通过电子邮件进行查找。
 与所有其他冠状病毒测试一样，该非处方药盒旨在帮助阻止该疾病的传播。当被感染的人被告知自己的病情时，他们可以寻求治疗并开始隔离，从而防止其他人染上疾病。
 对于未投保的人，在家中进行的冠状病毒测试是免费的，并且如果开具保险单，则无需支付任何前期费用。根据Pixel网站的说法，如果客户选择自费支付，则价格为119美元。
 一名医生因病毒而抱怨缺乏ICU病床。然后他说他的医院把他拒之门外。
  克利文·吉尔曼（Cleavon Gilman）经历了三次冠状病毒激增，但急诊室医生于11月22日达到了临界点。这位医生刚到亚利桑那州尤马的一家医院进行了12小时的轮班，不久就意识到该设施无处可去派出三名ICU患者。 
因此，他在推特上写道：“刚上班，就被告知亚利桑那州没有ICU病床了。”
 鸣叫迅速疯传，有助于推动吉尔曼这样突出当选总统拜登后来打电话来感谢他的宣传。不过，他说，第二天，医院告诉第三方公司，他一直在努力，不想让他返回。 41岁的吉尔曼（Gilman）说，自11月23日以来，他一直没有工作。
 据《布鲁塞尔时报》报道，比利时佛兰德地区的一些警察将使用无人机-并计算比萨饼盒的交付量-以防止年末庆祝活动。
 随着圣诞节和除夕的临近，比利时国家政府已通知居民，圣诞节前夕，他们最多可以接待四位客人。那些单独居住的人可以在圣诞节前夕和圣诞节最多接待两位客人。
 但是在法兰德斯东部的卡尔马，警察将依靠带有扩音器和热敏摄像头的无人机来监视是否有违反烟花爆竹和限制聚会的法规。
 在安特卫普省，警察可能会保留订购私人房屋的披萨盒数量，以作为内部聚会的可能指示。
 如果被违反冠状病毒规则的警务人员或无人驾驶飞机抓获，居民将被罚款300美元。 
春季，无人驾驶飞机飞过卡玛（Carma）的天空，以执行大流行限制；去年圣诞节和新年期间，无人驾驶飞机执行烟花禁令。
  弗拉芒自由开放自由党的蒂姆•范德普（Tim Vandeput）表示：“严格的规则对于打击病毒的传播和保护我们的健康是必不可少的，但这些控制措施必须保持比例，并尊重基本原则，例如隐私权和房屋的不可侵犯性。”告诉《布鲁塞尔时报》。
 现在，在美国死于冠状病毒的covid-19致死人数超过了美国历史上第二场最致命的战争中在战斗中丧生的美国军人人数。
 退伍军人事务部估计，在第二次世界大战期间，有291,500多名美国士兵在战斗中丧生。据《华盛顿邮报》的追踪者称，与冠状病毒相关的死亡超过了星期四的总数，达到了291,800多人。
 冠状病毒死亡人数也比南北战争中的战斗死亡人数高，是第一次世界大战期间死亡人数的五倍多，几乎是自1775年以来所有美国战争中约651,000例战斗死亡的一半。
 为了进一步比较，疾病控制和预防中心估计，美国每年有12,000至61,000人死于流感。
 德国周五报告了创纪录的每日新的冠状病毒病例，因为政府官员准备在圣诞节前开会讨论扩大限制措施。 
据德国传染病机构罗伯特·科赫研究所（Robert Koch Institute）称，周五每天的日冕病毒病例数首次达到近30,000例。该国连续第二天录得近600例冠状病毒死亡。
 德国本周的冠状病毒死亡人数超过20,000。已有超过120万人感染了该病毒。
 德国因对这种大流行的反应而受到早期赞扬。与欧洲邻国如法国或意大利相比，它的死亡人数更少，前科学家总理安格拉·默克尔（Angela Merkel）被认为是稳定的领导风格。
 自11月初以来，德国一直处于部分封锁状态，当时餐馆和酒吧关闭，建议居民在家工作。
 但是在本周，默克尔向德国人发出了慷慨激昂的呼吁，以进一步减少他们的社交活动，并采取额外的预防措施来遏制该病毒的传播。
 默克尔在本周早些时候向国会发表的讲话中说：“每天有五百人死亡是不可接受的。”
 她说：“如果现在圣诞节之前我们之间的联系太多，而这最终是与祖父母的最后一个圣诞节，那么从某种意义上说，我们一定会失败，”她说。 
默克尔赞同科学咨询机构的建议，要求进一步限制公共生活，包括关闭学校并命令非必要的企业停业至1月10日。她将在周末与德国联邦政府领导人会面，讨论可能的新限制，德意志威尔报告。
 特朗普将疫苗分配交给拜登：“我不这么认为”
  特朗普总统上周五表示反对将疫苗分发给即将上任的拜登政府，他在推特上写道：“他们希望加入并接管现代历史上最伟大和最快的医学奇迹之一。”
 特朗普补充说：“我不这么认为。”特朗普正在推动最高法院推翻选举结果。
 就在几天前，卫生与公共服务部长亚历克斯·阿扎尔（Alex Azar）在电视采访中承诺与拜登过渡小组尽可能无缝地合作，以挽救生命，特朗普发表上述言论。
 特朗普在推文中声称，奥巴马-拜登政府对“猪流感”的反应是“一场彻底的全面灾难”，这表明拜登政府没有资格监督冠状病毒疫苗的分发。
 在周五早些时候的一条推文中，特朗普对疫苗批准程序表示不耐烦，称食品药品监督管理局是“一只大而又旧的慢乌龟”，同时劝告该机构的领导人斯蒂芬·哈恩“立即拿出大坝疫苗。 ”他补充说：“停止玩游戏，开始挽救生命！！！” 
在特朗普发表推文时，阿扎尔（Azar）在美国广播公司（ABC）的《早安美国》（Good Morning America）采访中说，美国食品药品管理局（FDA）已告知辉瑞（Pfizer）“他们确实打算进行疫苗授权。”
 美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）已通知辉瑞制药公司及其合作伙伴BioNTech，该公司计划“迅速致力于最终确定和签发”其针对冠状病毒的疫苗的紧急使用授权。
 卫生与公共服务部长亚历克斯·阿扎尔（Alex Azar）周五在美国广播公司（ABC）的“早安美国”节目中接受采访时说，人们最早可以在星期一或星期二在美国开始接种该疫苗。
 周四，一个咨询委员会建议FDA批准该疫苗用于紧急用途，因为该国冠状病毒病例激增。
 该机构表示，它还通知了美国疾病控制和预防与行动中心Warp Speed，这是加速疫苗开发的公私合作伙伴关系，以便``他们可以执行及时分发疫苗的计划。''
 特朗普总统在推特上推特上周五在推特上批评FDA“官僚主义”，敦促该机构专员斯蒂芬·哈恩（Stephen Hahn）加快疫苗分发过程。
 特朗普在推特上写道：“现在就拿出大坝疫苗，汉恩博士。” “停止玩游戏并开始挽救生命！” 
法国下午8点通过。在节假日（包括除夕）宵禁
  法国总理让·卡斯特克斯（Jean Castex）宣布，法国将在晚上8点前取代旅行限制。星期二实行宵禁，这样人们就可以在假期里探望家人，同时仍在尝试防止新型冠状病毒的传播。
 Castex说，宵禁不会阻止人们在圣诞节前夕离开家，而是在整个假期期间，包括除夕，都将实行宵禁。大多数娱乐场所，包括博物馆和电影院，也将保持关闭。
 法国在10月下旬进入第二次全国封锁，原因是冠状病毒死亡人数大幅增加。去年11月，该国每天报告500多人死亡，是全球第三高的死亡人数。自2月以来，法国有57,000多人死亡。
 法国总统马克龙（Emmanuel Macron）上个月表示，官员们希望在假期前放宽限制，但新的冠状病毒病例数量并未减少到足以使室内娱乐恢复的程度。该国的目标是将新病例减少到每天少于5,000，但在过去一周中，平均每天要增加两倍以上。
 法国24日报道，卡斯特克斯说：“我们处于一个平稳的时期。” “我们不能放松警惕。”
 由于这些冠状病毒的数量，卡斯特克斯周四表示，原定于下周到期的一些限制将延长至少三周。 
莫斯科—俄罗斯人造卫星V疫苗的开发者周五宣布，阿斯利康已同意尝试使用一种人造卫星V组分作为研发改良疫苗的一部分。
 Sputnik V的开发人员在推特上写道：“疫苗合作的新篇章从今天开始。” “我们提出了要约，阿斯利康接受了。”
 英国瑞典公司阿斯利康（AstraZeneca）证实，它将很快与Gaputya研究所Sputnik V的开发者一起探讨两种常见的基于感冒病毒的疫苗是否可以协同工作。
 它说，阿斯利康正在研究如何评估不同疫苗的组合，“并将很快与俄罗斯的加马利亚研究所一起探索，以了解两种腺病毒疫苗能否成功组合。”
 Sputnik V疫苗正在第三阶段试验完成之前在俄罗斯推广给卫生工作者和学校教师。
 Sputnik V的开发人员在周五还提议与法国的赛诺菲和英国的GSK分享俄罗斯疫苗，以提高其疫苗的功效。遭受挫折的是，赛诺菲和葛兰素史克上周五宣布，由于第一阶段和第二阶段试验的结果发现老年人的免疫应答较低，他们的疫苗将推迟到明年年底。
 在完成3期试验后，牛津大学和阿斯利康公司发现他们的疫苗有效率为70％，并质疑它在55岁以上人群中的效果如何。 
据监测该疫苗临床试验数据的独立委员会成员拉米尔·哈夫列夫（Ramil Khabriev）称，两剂后俄罗斯人造卫星V疫苗的功效为96.5％。据俄罗斯塔斯社报道，他引用了有关三期试验的最新初步数据。
 他说，在第一次注射疫苗后，包括两次注射，Sputnik V疫苗在69.3％的病例中有效。尽管官方表示尚未观察到有害影响，但尚未公布详细数据。
 共和党控制的新罕布什尔州众议院议员担心，在新宣誓就职的发言人于周三去世后，他们可能已暴露于冠状病毒。
 71岁的迪克·辛奇（Dick Hinch）在新罕布什尔大学（University of New Hampshire）在户外有400人的州议会就职典礼后一周死于covid-19。
 只有270名代表参加了该活动，由于冠状病毒问题，许多民主党人跳过了该活动。在活动期间，一些与会者被拍照时没有面具，尽管大多数人似乎都遮住了脸。欣奇戴着口罩，但照片显示，他在活动期间在讲台上讲话时滑到了鼻子下方。
 州长克里斯·苏努努（R）在新闻发布会上说：“你知道，迪克（Dick）的逝世是真实的-悲惨的是，我们所有人都为之伤心-这确实是一个警告信号，表明我们距离这一目标还远。星期四。
 据《 WMUR 9》报道，11月20日在麦金太尔滑雪场举行的室内核心小组会议之后，在上周的活动之前，一些GOP立法者对冠状病毒进行了阳性检测。 
新罕布什尔州民主党人要求该州为所有想要接受筛选的工作人员以及参加上周在新罕布什尔大学举行的活动的每位立法者提供测试。
 该党在写给州长的一封信中说：“任何人在任何工作中都不必担心自己在工作中的健康。”
 欣奇的去世导致新罕布什尔州的一些议员批评在大流行期间反对口罩规定和其他冠状病毒限制的同事。
 “我相信共和党那些拒绝采取合理预防措施的人所施加的同伴压力是导致希钦议长过世的最终原因，”共和党人，退休医生威廉·马什（William Marsh）周四对美联社表示。
 全世界大约70％的医护人员是女性，这意味着许多人处于冠状病毒大流行的最前线，并且从理论上讲，在她们开始分发疫苗时，他们正朝着最先打针。
 但是有一个陷阱：从俄罗斯到英国的疫苗接种计划不包括正在怀孕，母乳喂养或计划怀孕的妇女，因为在最初的试验中并未对这些人群进行临床测试。
 周二，英国使用辉瑞（Pfizer）-生物技术公司（BioNTech）注射疫苗启动了西方国家的首例大规模冠状病毒疫苗接种。但是到目前为止，英国政府的指导已将孕妇排除在外。

The Washington Post is providing this important information about the coronavirus for free.  For more free coverage of the coronavirus pandemic,  sign up for our Coronavirus Updates newsletter where all stories are free to read.
 The coronavirus will kill more people in the United States every day for the next two to three months than died in the attacks on Sept. 11, 2001, or Pearl Harbor, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Director Robert Redfield said Thursday.
  The stark warning came as  the United States set a record for fatalities for the second day in a row, surpassing 3,300 deaths Thursday and bringing  the U.S. death toll to more than 291,800, which is more than the number of U.S. soldiers  killed in combat during World War II.
 Sign up for our coronavirus newsletter | Mapping the spread of the coronavirus:  Across the U.S. |  Worldwide |  Vaccine tracker | The  covid-19 symptoms to watch for
 Sweden mandates distance learning for students 14 to 16 in the country’s first move to remote learning
  Sweden — once the poster child for not shutting down schools or requiring masks as the coronavirus spread — is moving all students ages 14 to 16 in the capital to remote learning for the remainder of the year as cases there rise.
 “We must drastically reduce our contacts, so unfortunately I am forced to recommend that (Stockholm region) municipalities end on-site teaching for students in advanced stage education starting on Monday,” regional infections control chief Maria Rotzen Ostlund said in a statement,  Reuters reported.
 The shift marks Sweden’s first foray into distance learning, which last spring quickly became the norm across Europe as the coronavirus spread. Sweden initially touted its contrarian approach to managing the pandemic, which favored keeping its economy open and relying on citizens to keep themselves safe. But it has since reverted to some partial restrictions as infections and fatalities persist.
 The capital, Stockholm, has been hardest hit by covid-19, suffering a third of the country’s more than 7,500 coronavirus-related deaths, according to Reuters.
 A prominent nonpartisan watchdog group has expressed concern to federal officials about allegations that the director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention  ordered the destruction of a politically meaningful email.
 CDC Director Robert R. Redfield came under fire Thursday from Rep. James E. Clyburn (D-S.C.), who alleged that Redfield tried to destroy evidence of a top Trump administration health official inappropriately interfering in a report on the coronavirus’s risk to children. The accusation stems from an interview in which the editor of the CDC’s Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report said she was told to delete an email from a senior adviser to Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar.
 The watchdog group, Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington, wrote to the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA)  to allege that the CDC had violated the Federal Records Act by not reporting the matter to the national archivist. CREW asked the National Archives to “take appropriate corrective action,” including directing the CDC to issue a report on the situation.
 “NARA now more than ever must promote transparency and ensure that agencies are complying with record-keeping laws,” CREW Executive Director Noah Bookbinder said in  a statement. “The lives of Americans literally depend upon our access to scientifically accurate information.”
 Redfield has said he “instructed CDC staff to ignore” the email from Azar’s adviser, Paul Alexander.
 TORONTO — Unprecedented restrictions on all nonessential travel at U.S. land borders with Canada and Mexico will enter their 10th month, officials said Friday.
 The measures, which will be in place until at least Jan. 21, were imposed in mid-March and have been extended in 30-day increments since.
 They’ve had limited impact on trade and the movement of essential workers, but they have hurt tourism and upended life in close-knit border towns.
 Chad Wolf, the acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, confirmed the extension of the measures in tweets.
 “As this administration continues to make great progress on a vaccine for covid,” he  said on Twitter, “we will reevaluate nonessential travel restrictions again early in the new year.”
 Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau  said this month that the border curbs won’t be relaxed “until the virus is significantly more under control everywhere around the world,” suggesting that the measures will be in place well into 2021.
 The Food and Drug Administration has authorized the first direct to consumer test for the coronavirus, allowing Americans to take their own nasal swab and send the sample in for lab testing without a prescription.
 While doctors can prescribe home testing for the coronavirus after a brief questionnaire with patients, the FDA’s approval on Wednesday removes that required step for LabCorp’s Pixel COVID-19 Test Home Collection Kit.
 “With the first over-the-counter at-home collection kit ever authorized by the FDA for COVID-19, we are empowering people to learn about their health and make confident decisions,” Brian Caveney, chief medical officer and president of LabCorp Diagnostics, said in a statement. “With this authorization, we can help more people get tested, reduce the spread of the virus and improve the health of our communities.”
 People 18 years or older who’d like to order the test can purchase it online or in stores without a doctor’s permission. After collecting their own sample through the nose, patients send the material to LabCorp for processing. A health-care provider will contact the patient if the test results are positive or invalid. If the test is negative, patients will find out through LabCorp’s online portal or through email.
 As with all other coronavirus tests, the over-the-counter kit is designed to help stop the spread of the disease. When infected people are alerted about their condition, they can seek treatment and begin to isolate, preventing other people from contracting the illness.
 The at-home coronavirus test is free for the uninsured and has no upfront costs if it is billed to insurance. If customers choose to pay out of pocket, it runs $119, according to Pixel’s website.
 A doctor went viral decrying a lack of ICU beds. Then he says his hospital shut him out.
  Cleavon Gilman has worked through three coronavirus surges, but the emergency room doctor reached his breaking point on Nov. 22. The physician had just arrived at a Yuma, Ariz., hospital for a 12-hour shift, and soon realized the facility had nowhere to send three ICU patients.
 So, he  tweeted: “Just got to work and was notified there are no more ICU beds in the state of Arizona.”
 The tweet swiftly went viral, helping to propel Gilman to such prominence that President-elect Joe Biden later called to thank him for his advocacy. The next day, though, he says the hospital told the third-party company that he works through that it didn’t want him to return. Gilman, 41, says he’s been kept out of work since Nov. 23.
 Some police in Belgium’s Flanders region will be using drones — and counting pizza box deliveries — to prevent end-of-the-year celebrations,  according to the Brussels Times.
 As Christmas and New Year’s Eve approach, the national Belgian government has told residents that they can have up to four guests gathered outside on Christmas Eve. Those who live alone can host up to two guests on both Christmas Eve and Christmas Day.
 But in Carma, eastern Flanders, police will rely on drones with megaphones and heat-sensing cameras to monitor for any violators of regulations barring fireworks and limiting gatherings.
 In Antwerp province, police may keep tabs on the number of pizza boxes ordered to private homes as possible indicators of a gathering inside.
 If caught by a police officer or drone contravening coronavirus rules, residents could be fined $300.
 Drones were flown above Carma’s skies in the spring to enforce pandemic restrictions, as well as last year during the Christmas and New Year’s period to enforce a fireworks ban.
  “Strict rules are necessary to combat the spread of the virus and protect our health, but these controls must remain proportional and respect fundamental principles such as the right to privacy and the inviolability of the home,” Tim Vandeput of the Flemish liberal Open VLD party  told the Brussels Times.
 The number of people in the United States who have died of covid-19, the illness caused by the coronavirus, is now greater than the number of U.S. service members who died in combat during the second deadliest war in American history.
 More than 291,500 U.S. soldiers died in battle during World War II, the Department of Veterans Affairs estimates. Coronavirus-related deaths surpassed that total Thursday to reach more than 291,800, according to The Washington Post’s  tracker.
 The coronavirus death toll is also higher than the combat deaths in the Civil War, more than five times as high as those in World War I and nearly half as many as the roughly 651,000 combat deaths in all U.S. wars since 1775.
 For further comparison, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention estimates that between 12,000 and 61,000 people in the United States  die of influenza each year.
 Germany reported  a record number of daily new coronavirus cases on Friday, as government officials were set to meet to discuss expanding restrictions ahead of the Christmas holidays.
 According to the Robert Koch Institute, Germany’s infectious-disease agency, the number of daily coronavirus cases reached nearly 30,000 for the first time on Friday. For the second day in a row, the country recorded close to 600 coronavirus deaths.
 Germany’s coronavirus death toll surpassed 20,000 this week. More than 1.2 million people have been infected with the virus.
 Germany was praised early on for its response to the pandemic. It suffered fewer deaths than European neighbors such as France or Italy, and Chancellor Angela Merkel, a former scientist, was  credited for a steady leadership style.
 Germany has been under a partial lockdown since early November, when restaurants and bars closed and residents were advised to work from home.
 But this week, Merkel made an impassioned plea to Germans to reduce their social engagements further and to take extra precautions to help curb the spread of the virus.
 “Five hundred deaths a day is unacceptable,” Merkel said in a speech to parliament earlier this week.
 “If we now, just before Christmas, have too many contacts and this ends up being the last Christmas with the grandparents, then we will have failed in a sense,” she said, according to German broadcaster Deutsche Welle.
 Merkel endorsed recommendations from a scientific advisory body for further restrictions on public life, including closing schools and ordering nonessential businesses to shut until Jan. 10. She will meet with the leaders of Germany’s federal states over the weekend to discuss potential new restrictions, Deutsche Welle reported.
 Trump on turning over vaccine distribution to Biden: ‘I don’t think so’
  President Trump on Friday voiced resistance to turning over distribution of vaccines to the incoming Biden administration, writing on Twitter that “they want to come in and take over one of the ‘greatest and fastest medical miracles in modern day history.’”
 “I don’t think so,” added Trump, who is pushing the Supreme Court to overturn the election results.
 Trump’s comments came just days after Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar pledged in a television interview to work with the Biden transition team as seamlessly as possible with the aim of saving lives.
 In his tweet, Trump claimed that the response to the “swine flu” during the Obama-Biden administration was “a complete and total disaster” — suggesting that should disqualify the Biden administration from overseeing distribution of coronavirus vaccines.
 In an earlier tweet Friday, Trump expressed impatience with the vaccine approval process, writing that the Food and Drug Administration is “a big, old, slow turtle” while exhorting the agency’s leader, Stephen Hahn, to “get the dam vaccines out NOW.” He added: “Stop playing games and start saving lives!!!”
 As Trump was tweeting, Azar said in an interview on ABC’s “Good Morning America” that the FDA has informed Pfizer that “they do intend to proceed towards an authorization of their vaccine.”
 The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has  notified the Pfizer pharmaceutical company and its partner, BioNTech, that it plans to “rapidly work toward finalization and issuance” of an emergency-use authorization for their vaccine against the coronavirus.
 Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar said in  an interview on ABC’s “Good Morning America” on Friday that people could begin receiving the vaccine in the United States as early as Monday or Tuesday.
 An advisory committee on Thursday recommended that the FDA approve the vaccine for emergency use, as coronavirus cases soar across the country.
 The agency said that it also notified the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and Operation Warp Speed, the public-private partnership to accelerate vaccine development, so that “they can execute their plans for timely vaccine distribution.”
 On Twitter, President Trump  criticized the FDA as “heavily bureaucratic” on Friday, urging the agency’s commissioner, Stephen Hahn, to speed up the process to distribute vaccines.
 “Get the dam vaccines out NOW, Dr. Hahn,”  Trump tweeted. “Stop playing games and start saving lives!!!”
 France adopts an 8 p.m. curfew over the holidays, including New Year’s Eve
  France’s Prime Minister Jean Castex announced that the nation would replace travel restrictions with an 8 p.m. curfew on Tuesday, so that people can visit family members over the holidays while still attempting to prevent the spread of the novel coronavirus.
 The curfew will not prevent people from leaving their homes on Christmas Eve, but will be in place over the rest of the holiday season, including New Year’s Eve, Castex said. Most entertainment venues, including museums and movie theaters, will also remain closed.
 France had entered a  second nationwide lockdown in late October as coronavirus deaths increased significantly. In November, the nation was reporting  more than 500 deaths a day, the third-highest toll worldwide. More than 57,000 people have  died in France since February.
 French President Emmanuel Macron said last month that officials  hoped to loosen restrictions ahead of the holidays, but the number of new coronavirus cases has not declined enough to allow indoor entertainment to resume. The nation had aimed to reduce new cases to less than 5,000 a day, but was averaging  more than twice that number each day over the last week.
 “We’re on a sort of plateau,” Castex said Thursday,  France 24 reported. “We cannot let our guard down.”
 Because of those coronavirus numbers, Castex said Thursday that some of the restrictions that had been set to expire next week would be extended for at least three more weeks.
 MOSCOW — The developers of Russia’s Sputnik V vaccine announced Friday that AstraZeneca had agreed to try using one Sputnik V component as part of efforts to create an improved vaccine.
 “The new chapter of vaccine cooperation has started today,” the developers of Sputnik V  tweeted. “We made an offer and AstraZeneca accepted it.”
 British-Swedish company AstraZeneca  confirmed it would soon begin exploring with the developers of Sputnik V, the Gamaleya Institute, whether two common cold virus-based vaccines could work together.
 It said AstraZeneca was looking at how to assess combinations of different vaccines “and will soon begin exploring with Gamaleya Research Institute in Russia to understand whether two adenovirus-based vaccines can be successfully combined.”
 The Sputnik V vaccine is being  rolled out in Russia to health workers and schoolteachers before the completion of Phase 3 trials.
 Sputnik V’s developers on Friday also offered to share the Russian vaccine with France’s Sanofi and Britain’s GSK to boost their vaccine’s efficacy. In a setback, Sanofi and GSK had  announced on Friday that their vaccine would be delayed until the end of next year after results of Phase 1 and 2 trials found a low immune response in older adults.
 After completion of Phase 3 trials, Oxford University and AstraZeneca found their vaccine’s efficacy was 70 percent, with  questions about how well it worked in people over 55.
 The efficacy of Russia’s Sputnik V vaccine after two doses is 96.5 percent, according to Ramil Khabriev, a member of the independent committee for monitoring data from clinical trials of the vaccine. He was citing the latest preliminary data on the Phase 3 trial, according to Russia’s Tass news agency.
 After the first shot of the vaccine, which consists of two injections, Sputnik V was effective in 69.3 percent of cases, he said. Detailed data has not yet been released, although officials say no harmful effects have been observed.
 Members of the Republican-controlled New Hampshire House of Representatives fear they may have been exposed to the coronavirus after the newly sworn-in speaker died on Wednesday.
 Dick Hinch (R), 71, died of covid-19 a week after the 400-person State House had its swearing-in ceremony outdoors at the University of New Hampshire.
 Only 270 representatives attended the event, with many Democrats skipping it because of coronavirus concerns. Some attendees were  photographed without masks during the event, though most people appeared to be covering their faces. Hinch wore a mask, but photos showed it slipping below his nose as he spoke at a podium during the event.
 “You know, Dick’s passing is a real — as tragic as it is, as heartbroken as we all are — it really is a warning sign that we are far from over this,” Gov. Chris Sununu (R) said at a news conference Thursday.
 Before last week’s event, several GOP legislators tested positive for coronavirus after an indoor caucus meeting at the McIntyre Ski Area on Nov. 20,  WMUR 9 reported.
 New Hampshire Democrats asked that the state provide testing for any staffers who want to be screened and for every legislator who attended last week’s event at the University of New Hampshire.
 “No one, in any job, should have to fear for their health at work,” the party  said in a letter to the governor.
 Hinch’s death led some New Hampshire lawmakers to criticize their colleagues who have opposed mask mandates and other coronavirus restrictions during the pandemic.
 “I believe the peer pressure exerted by those in the Republican Party who refuse to take reasonable precautions is the ultimate cause of Speaker Hinch’s passing,” Rep. William Marsh, who is a Republican and a retired doctor,  told the Associated Press Thursday.
 Around 70 percent of health-care workers worldwide are women, meaning many are on the front lines of the  coronavirus pandemic and, in theory, toward the front of the line for a vaccine shot as they begin to be distributed.
 But there’s a catch: Vaccination programs from Russia to Britain are excluding women who are pregnant, breastfeeding or planning for a pregnancy because the vaccine candidates were not clinically tested on these populations as part of their initial trials.
 On Tuesday, Britain launched  the West’s first mass coronavirus vaccinations using the Pfizer-BioNTech shot. But guidance so far from the British government has excluded pregnant women from the shots.

  Datasette面向数据记者，博物馆策展人，档案管理员，地方政府以及任何希望与世界分享数据的人。它是更广泛的工具和插件生态系统的一部分，这些工具和插件致力于使结构化数据尽可能高效地工作。

  数据集发布使您可以立即将数据发布到托管提供商，例如Google Cloud Run，Heroku或Vercel。

note_en:
Datasette is a tool for exploring and publishing data. It helps people take data of any shape or size, analyze and explore it, and publish it as an interactive website and accompanying API.
  Datasette is aimed at data journalists, museum curators, archivists, local governments and anyone else who has data that they wish to share with the world. It is part of a  wider ecosystem of tools and plugins dedicated to making working with structured data as productive as possible.
  Try a demo and explore 33,000 power plants around the world, then take a look at  some other examples of Datasette in action.
  Import data from CSVs, JSON, database connections and more. Datasette will automatically show you patterns in your data and help you share your findings with your colleagues.
  datasette publish lets you instantly publish your data to hosting providers like Google Cloud Run, Heroku or Vercel.
  Spin up a JSON API for any data in minutes. Use it to prototype and prove your ideas without building a custom backend.
  Datasette 0.53 -  datasette serve --create option,  ?column__arraynotcontains= table filter,  ?_header=off CSV option and updated links to reflect the new project website.
  New Datasette project website - three years after the first release Datasette finally has its own website! The site includes project  news, a  plugin directory,  use-cases and  examples. The site is implemented using Datasette custom templates, and the source code is available  on GitHub.
    Datasette 0.52 -  --config is now  --setting, new  database_actions plugin hook,  datasette publish cloudrun --apt-get-install option and several bug fixes.
    Datasette 0.51 - A new visual design, plugin hooks for adding navigation options, better handling of binary data, URL building utility methods and better support for running Datasette behind a proxy.  Annotated release notes.
    Datasette 0.50 - New column actions menu.  datasette.client object for plugins to make internal API requests. Improved documentation on deploying Datasette.  Annotated release notes.
    Datasette 0.49 - JSON API for writable canned queries, path parameters for custom pages. See also  Datasette 0.49: The annotated release notes.
    Datasette 0.48 - Documentation now lives at  docs.datasette.io, improvements to the  extra_template_vars,  extra_css_urls,  extra_js_urls and  extra_body_script plugin hooks.
    Datasette 0.47 - Datasette can now be installed using Homebrew!  brew install simonw/datasette/datasette. Also new:  datasette install name-of-plugin and  datasette uninstall name-of-plugin commands, and  datasette --get '/-/versions.json' to output the result of Datasette HTTP calls on the command-line.
    Datasette 0.46 - security fix relating to CSRF protection for writable canned queries, a new logo, new debugging tools, improved file downloads and more.
        Two new plugins:  datasette-copyable and datasette-insert-api.  datasette-copyable adds copy-and-paste export options, and  datasette-insert-api lets you create tables and insert or update data by POSTing JSON directly to Datasette.
    Datasette 0.45 -  Magic parameters for canned queries, a log out feature, improved plugin documentation and four new plugin hooks. See also  Datasette 0.45: The annotated release notes.
        Datasette 0.44 -  Authentication and permissions,  writable canned queries, flash messages, new plugin hooks and much, much more.
  Fixed bug where users with a  "someusername_password_hash" value defined in  metadata.json but no corresponding  "actor" record were incorrectly signed in as an actor with  "id": "username", when they should have been signed in as an actor with  "id": "someusername". #14
    Datasette has an official project website now, at  https://datasette.io/. This release mainly updates the documentation to reflect the new site.
 datasette serve has a new  --create option, which will create blank database files if they do not already exist rather than exiting with an error. ( #1135)
 New  ?_header=off option for CSV export which omits the CSV header row,  documented here. ( #1133)
  Project news no longer lives in the README - it can now be found at  https://datasette.io/news. ( #1137)
                    Chart rendering now takes place in a subprocess with a 5 second default time limit. Time limit can be customized using a new  render_time_limit plugin configuration option. #4
      Errors output by Datasette (e.g. for invalid SQL queries) now go to  stderr, not  stdout. ( #1131)
 Fix for a startup error on windows caused by unnecessary  from os import EX_CANTCREAT - thanks, Abdussamet Koçak. ( #1094)
      Form inputs are now wrapped in a paragraph, making form less cramped when displayed next to metadata. #12
      Fixed bug where static assets would 404 for Datasette installed on ARM Amazon Linux. ( #1124)
    Error message if you attempt to open a SpatiaLite database now suggests using  --load-extension=spatialite if it detects that the extension is available in a common location. ( #1115)
  Databases larger than 32MB that are published to Cloud Run can now be downloaded. ( #749)

note:
在迪士尼投资者日期间发布的许多花絮中，有很长的一段是为洛基（Loki）宣传的预告片，其中包括汤姆·希德勒斯顿（Tom Hiddleston）具有超凡魅力的骗子半神和（收养）兄弟（托尔）的漫威限量版在《复仇者联盟：残局》中。 Marvel Studios发誓要提供“沉重”的游戏。它的各种有限系列的预算，例如WandaVision（也有新的预告片），这款Loki宣传片肯定具有很高的电影制作价值。漫威影业（Marvel Studios）宣布将于2018年为迪士尼+开发各种限量系列，所有这些系列均以电影中的配角为特色，这些人物在MCEU中未获得独立电影的支持。大部分细节都被严格保密，但是我们知道，Loki和WandaVision都将与即将出版的《疯狂的宇宙》中的奇异博士联系在一起。粉丝最喜欢的Loki一直是最多层，心理上复杂的角色之一，因此我们非常希望看到限量系列为他准备的东西。
 Loki的首席作家Michael Waldron（Rick和Morty）在三月份的播客采访中谈到了这一点。 “我认为这是与身份，你是谁，你想成为谁的斗争。我真的很喜欢为控制而战的角色，” Waldron说。 “当然，您会发现在Loki的前十年电影中，他在生活的各个关键部分都失去了控制，他被收养，一切都变得如此，这通过对家人的愤怒和恶意表现出来。”
 洛基（Loki）在今年的超级碗比赛中播出的简短镜头显示，他身穿带有首字母＆＃34; TVA＆＃34;的连身裤。踩在上面。顽固的Marvel粉丝会认出这个缩写词：它代表时间差异管理局，即所谓的“时间管理者”。在MCU中，他们监视违反时间轴的行为。通过尝试更改历史记录来吸引他们的注意，您可能会遇到追溯大炮（Ret Con）的错误提示，并将整个历史记录从历史时间轴中删除。
    时空旅行很可能在Loki中扮演重要角色，这部预告片在很大程度上证实了这一点。它以倒叙开始，回味到《残局》中的关键场景，2012年的洛基（Loki）抓住包含太空石的tesseract，并通过门户消失。接下来，我们在一个荒芜的沙漠中看到他，当地居民有些困惑。切入场景中的是欧文·威尔逊（Owen Wilson）仍不为人知的角色，坐在电梯里-他绝对是TVA，因为洛基（Loki）穿着他的囚犯连身衣，威尔逊（Wilson）在场景中也承认了这一点（＆＃ 34;时间在TVA中流逝的时间有所不同）。
 洛基（Loki）假定他将被处决，但威尔逊（Wilson）的性格向他保证，他只想讲话。洛基（Loki）声称他不喜欢说话，但是，那真是个谎言。 （我们俩都知道您喜欢交谈，威尔逊的角色在偷偷摸摸。＆＃34;说话健谈...＆＃34;）其余的预告片是一堆混乱的场面大部分是叙述性的线索，但显然TVA将迫使Loki面对他所造成的损害。
 Loki目前定于2021年5月21日在Disney +上发行。总而言之，这对角色来说似乎是一次了不起的新冒险，我们正在计算直到那时的日子。
note_en:
Among the  many (many!) tidbits  unveiled during Disney's Investor Day was a lengthy teaser trailer for   Loki, the Marvel spinoff limited series featuring Tom Hiddleston's charismatic trickster demigod and (adoptive) brother to Thor, last seen in  Avengers: Endgame. Marvel Studios had vowed to provide "hefty" budgets for its various limited series, like  WandaVision (which also  has a new trailer), and this  Loki teaser definitely has the high cinematic production values to show for it.  Marvel Studios announced the development of its various limited series for Disney+ in 2018, all featuring supporting characters from the films who were not given their own standalone films within the MCEU. Details have largely been kept tightly under wraps, but we do know that both  Loki and  WandaVision will tie into the forthcoming  Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness. Fan favorite Loki has always been one of the most multi-layered, psychologically complex characters, so we're keen to see what the limited series has in store for him.
 Loki's head writer, Michael Waldron ( Rick and Morty), talked a little bit about that in a podcast interview back in March. “I think it’s the struggle with identity, who you are, who you want to be. I’m really drawn to characters who are fighting for control,”  said Waldron. “Certainly, you see that with Loki over the first ten years of movies, he’s out of control at pivotal parts of his life, he was adopted and everything, and that manifests itself through anger and spite towards his family.”
 A brief shot from  Loki that aired during the Super Bowl this year showed him wearing a jumpsuit with the initials "TVA" stamped on it. Hardcore Marvel fans will recognize the acronym: it stands for Time Variance Authority, the so-called "custodians of chronology" in the MCU, who monitor violations to the timeline. Catch their attention by trying to change history, and you just might meet the wrong end of the Retroactive Cannon (Ret Con) and have your entire history deleted from the historical timeline.
    Time travel will very likely play a significant role in  Loki, and this teaser trailer largely confirms that. It opens with a flashback to a pivotal scene in  Endgame, where 2012 Loki snags the tesseract containing the Space Stone and vanishes through a portal. We next see him in a barren desert, with some puzzled local denizens looking on. Cut to a scene with Owen Wilson's still-unidentified character in an elevator—he's definitely TVA, since Loki is wearing his prisoner's jumpsuit, and Wilson admits as much in the scene ("Time passes differently in the TVA").
 Loki assumes he's going to be executed, but Wilson's character assures him he just wants to talk. Loki claims he doesn't like to talk, but, well, that's a lie. ("We both know you  love to talk," Wilson's character snarks. "Talky talky... ") The rest of the trailer is a jumble of scenes without much of a narrative thread, but it does seem clear that the TVA will be forcing Loki to face up to the damage he has wrought.
 Loki is currently slated for release on Disney+ on May 21, 2021. All in all, it looks like a terrific new adventure for the character, and we're counting the days till then.
title: 随着迪士尼丢下冗长的预告片，我们对Loki有了第一次真正的了解
title_en: We’ve got our first real look at Loki as Disney drops lengthy teaser trailer

note:
我们知道它即将到来，现在我们得到了确认：即将推出新的《质量效应》游戏。它是在昨晚的Game Awards广播中用预告片宣布的。
  我们所拥有的是一部简短的预告片，没有任何游戏玩法，但仍然可以清楚地说明本系列的发展方向。新游戏将由BioWare在EA的保护下制造，最重要的启示是它似乎与原始游戏三部曲处于同一个星系中，它在《质量效应3》结束后立即出现，并且似乎至少要有一个返回字符（Liara T＆＃39; Soni）。至于游戏玩法是什么样的，或者故事从何而来，这是任何人的猜测。我们不想在这里破坏任何东西，但是与《三部曲》的出现相比，《质量效应3》的每个结局都无法识别游戏的设置，因此BioWare决定设置效果不佳的后续《质量效应：仙女座》在一个完全不同的银河系中出现了新角色。
 从新预告片中尚不清楚质量效应3的哪些结局以佳能结尾（考虑到某些结局的范围不同，玩家似乎不太可能导入保存或选择），但是在无论如何，预告片表明许多银河系已经毁了。
      值得一提的是，负责原始《质量效应》三部曲的几位关键创意团队成员已经从BioWare转移过来了，至少可以说，BioWare经历了几年的艰难历程。然而，根据项目总监Mike Gamble的推文，包括该叙事设计师Dusty Everman，艺术总监Derek Watts和电影导演Parrish Lay在内的少数人返回了该项目。
  无论新老成员的组成如何，考虑到《质量效应3》的不确定终结和对该游戏结论的强烈反对，制作新《质量效应》游戏的团队面临着艰巨的挑战。关于当今游戏行业在线毒性和开发人员骚扰的持续问题的持续讨论中出现了闪点，而且据报道，这是BioWare最初创始人离开该行业的主要因素。预告片没有透露游戏的发布日期或发布的平台。它甚至还没有给游戏起个名字，所以我们可能要等待一段时间才能获得更多信息。
note_en:
We knew it was  coming, and now we have confirmation: a new  Mass Effect game is on the horizon. It was announced with a teaser trailer during last night's Game Awards broadcast.
  What we have is a short trailer with no gameplay but one that still makes a few things clear about the direction of the series. The new game will be made by BioWare under the EA umbrella, and the foremost revelations are that it seems to be set in the same galaxy as the original trilogy of games, it occurs immediately after the conclusion of  Mass Effect 3, and it appears to feature at least one returning character (Liara T'Soni). As for what gameplay looks like or where the story goes from here, it's anyone's guess. We don't want to spoil anything here, but each of the  Mass Effect 3 endings left the game's setting unrecognizable compared to how it appeared during the course of the trilogy—hence BioWare's decision to set the  poorly received follow-up  Mass Effect: Andromeda in a completely different galaxy with a new cast of characters.
 It's not clear from the new trailer which of the  Mass Effect 3 endings ended up canon (given the scope of some endings' differences, it seems unlikely that the player will get to import saves or choose), but in any case, the trailer suggests that much of the galaxy is in ruin.
      It's worth noting that several key creative team members responsible for the original  Mass Effect trilogy have moved on from BioWare, which has seen a troubled few years with a rocky track record, to say the least. A select few, however, returned for this project, according to tweets  from Project Director Mike Gamble, including Narrative Designer Dusty Everman, Art Director Derek Watts, and Cinematics Director Parrish Lay.
  Regardless of its makeup of both new and old members, the team making the new  Mass Effect game has a difficult challenge ahead, given both the uncertain ending of  Mass Effect 3 and the harsh backlash to that game's conclusion, which was a flashpoint in the ongoing conversation about today's persistent problems of online toxicity and developer harassment in the gaming industry and reportedly a major factor in the original founders of BioWare leaving the industry. The trailer did not reveal a release date for the game or which platforms it will arrive on. It didn't even give the game a name yet, so we'll likely be waiting a while for more information.
title: “大众效应将重现：”预告片为续集设定了新的（旧的）方向
title_en: “Mass Effect Will Return:” Teaser trailer sets a new (old) direction for the sequel

For many of us, being home a lot more right now also means being online a lot more. It’s a great time to evaluate how you’re keeping your data safe — and to help others in your life do the same.
 Whether it’s teaching them to use things like physical security keys or just convincing them to stop writing their passwords on sticky notes, there are LOTS of little ways to nudge your friends and family in a safer direction. We’ve put together an array of gift ideas that’ll help them keep things locked down without breaking the bank (and, if you’re the one they’d call to help clean up after a security incident, probably save you some time in the end.)
  Much of what we wrote for  our 2019 guide still holds true. There are some timeless security essentials that you can’t miss out on, and we’ve searched around for the best deals. But we also have a few more fun gift ideas up our sleeves for the holiday season.
 This article contains links to affiliate partners where available. When you buy through these links, TechCrunch may earn an affiliate commission.
    Password managers are a real lifesaver. One strong, unique password lets you into your entire bank of passwords. They’re great for storing your passwords and other secrets, but also for encouraging you to use better, stronger, unique passwords. And because many are cross-platform, you can bring your passwords with you. Plenty of password managers exist — from  LastPass,  Lockbox, and  Dashlane, to open-source versions like KeePass. Many are free, but a premium subscription often comes with benefits and better features. And if you’re a journalist, 1Password has  a free subscription just for you.
 Price: Many free, premium offerings start at $36 – $45 annually  Available from:  1Password |  LastPass |  Dashlane |  KeePass
  Your online accounts have everything about you and you’d want to keep them safe. A security key is a physical hardware device that offers far greater protections than a two-factor code going to your phone. A security key plugs into your USB port on your computer (or  the charging port on your phone) to “prove” to online services, like Facebook, Google, and Twitter, that you are who you say you are.  YubiKeys are by far our favorite and come in all shapes and sizes. The latest YubiKey 5 series has something for everyone, no matter what kind of devices they have. They’re also cheap. Google also has a range of its own branded  Titan security keys, one of which also offers Bluetooth connectivity.
 Price: From $25 to $55, depending on device type, from  Yubico Store or  Google Store
  We’re all living through this pandemic together, and most of us are still working from home. If you, like me, have accidentally  joined a video call by mistake, you’ll be more thankful for your webcam cover than you could ever know. Webcam covers slide open when you need to access your camera, and slides to cover the lens when you don’t. It’s that easy. You can buy webcam covers from practically anywhere — just make sure you get a thin cover that supports your device so as to not damage its display when you close the lid. You can support local businesses and non-profits — you can search for unique and interesting webcam covers on Etsy or from your favorite internet rights group.
   Now you have your webcam cover, what about your microphone? Just as hackers can tap into your webcam, they can also pick up on your audio. Microphone blockers contain a semiconductor that tricks your computer or device into thinking that it’s a working microphone, when in fact it’s not able to pick up any audio. Anyone hacking into your device won’t hear a thing. Some modern Macs already come with  a new Apple T2 security chip which prevents hackers from snooping on your microphone when your laptop’s lid is shut. But a microphone blocker will work all the time, even when the lid is open.
       Think of a Pi-hole as a “hardware ad-blocker.” A Pi-hole is a modified Raspberry Pi mini-computer that runs ad-blocking technology as a box that sits on your network. It means that everyone on your home network benefits from ad blocking. Online ads generate revenue for websites but are also  notorious for tracking users across the web. Until ads can behave properly, a Pi-hole is a great way to capture and sinkhole bad ad traffic. The hardware may be cheap, but the ad-blocking software is free. Donations to the cause are welcome.
    How often have you seen someone’s private messages or documents as you look over their shoulder, or see them in the next seat over? It’s not as much of an issue when we’re all at home — but we’re also all getting pretty comfortable with having whatever on our screens right now, which might not be the best habit to develop. Privacy screens can help protect you from “visual hacking.” These screens make it near-impossible for anyone other than the device user to snoop at what you’re working on. And, you can get them for all kinds of devices and displays — including phones. But make sure you get the right size!
    Ditch the stereotypical hacker hoodie, and get some decent hacker apparel instead.  Zero Day Clothing has some excellent t-shirts and other clothing for your hacker or security-minded friends, from celebrating  Diffie-Hellman encryption,  fearing the botnet, or designing your own — there’s something for everyone. We’re particularly a fan because the company  supports a ton of causes, from net neutrality all the way to freedom of speech and LGBTQ+ rights and fair access to knowledge and education.
     The micro-computer maker has a new product,  the Raspberry Pi 400, a personal computer kit built into a keyboard and mouse. Connect it to a display and you can get started almost immediately. The device is more powerful than a Chromebook, and allows you to build, develop, and tinker to your heart’s content.
     Backdoors & Breaches is a security-focused card game, developed by  Black Hills Infosec, designed to help you conduct incident response exercises while learning about attack tactics, tools, and techniques. It’s a great way to learn more about how to respond after a breach. Don’t worry if you can’t play in person any time soon — you can still play virtually, thanks to an active Discord channel.

note:
今年，Google，Microsoft，Facebook和Amazon都努力地采用了新的云流技术，该技术将游戏处理和计算转移到基于云的服务器上，从而使用户可以在低功率系统上播放图形密集型内容或播放游戏而无需处理冗长的下载。
 高管们称这是游戏业的结构性转变，它将使获得下一代游戏的民主化。但是，在仔细研究围绕该技术构建的产品时，很难看到任何这些订阅服务都能成功的未来。
 游戏的大规模逐年变化很少，因为即使推出或发布了历史上独特的平台，大量开发人员也需要时间来聚集和采用新的东西，而用户需要更长的时间来合并。结果，即使在主要游戏机制造商推出历史上功能强大的硬件的一年中，大型科技巨头也将现金投入到新的云流技术中，而游戏玩家的集体记录时间却比以往任何时候都要长，但感觉好像并没有发生太大变化。
 也就是说，游戏产业在2020年确实突破了界限，尽管尚不清楚在何处获得有意义的基础。最雄心勃勃的驱动力是重新设计视频流网络图像中的市场，旨在通过推动以单人故事情节为基础的一次性购买来更协调地推动订阅增长，并远离数十年来定义的行业。互联网网络和即时支付基础设施软件的影响显着。
  但是，让游戏玩家远离一次性购买甚至不是游戏行业今年最基本的考虑，这是留给世界上最富有的公司协调行动以与无形竞争者抗衡游戏机战争的空间。毫不奇怪，这个领域中功能最全的产品来自于云服务的三重奏，最近几个月，Google，Microsoft和Amazon都取得了长足的进步。
 这种变化的驱动力既是虚拟桌面流媒体的成熟，又是开发人员继续向游戏平台之间的在线交叉玩法过渡，这一趋势长期以来一直被传统平台所有者抵制，以保持孤立的网络效应，促使游戏玩家购买与游戏机相同的游戏机。他们的朋友拥有。
 近年来，随着Epic Games的Fortnite之类的实体发展了庞大的用户群，这种交叉游戏的趋势达到了狂热的高度，这给开发人员带来了与平台所有者达成的交易的巨大影响力。 
尽管更深层次的交叉游戏的趋势为游戏界的新公司玩家播下了种子，但钱财最雄厚的高科技公司开创了最一致的游戏，将第三方候选人推向了游戏机大战。 。  对于许多游戏玩家来说，很清楚的是，即使在起步阶段，云游戏平台也无法满足他们的炒作，而独立的技术在技术上还不足以弥补内容库中明显缺乏选择的不足。
note_en:
This year, Google, Microsoft, Facebook and Amazon each leaned hard into new cloud-streaming tech that shifts game processing and computing to cloud-based servers, allowing users to play graphics-intensive content on low-powered systems or play titles without dealing with lengthy downloads.
 It was heralded by executives as a tectonic shift for gaming, one that would democratize access to the next generation of titles. But in taking a closer look at the products built around this tech, it’s hard to see a future where any of these subscription services succeed.
 Massive year-over-year changes in gaming are rare because even if a historically unique platform launches or is unveiled, it takes time for a critical mass of developers to congregate and adopt something new — and longer for users to coalesce. As a result, even in a year where major console makers launch historically powerful hardware, massive tech giants pump cash into new cloud-streaming tech and gamers log more hours collectively than ever before, it can feel like not much has shifted.
 That said, the gaming industry did push boundaries in 2020, though it’s unclear where meaningful ground was gained. The most ambitious drives were toward redesigning marketplaces in the image of video streaming networks, aiming to make a more coordinated move toward driving subscription growth and moving farther away from an industry defined for decades by one-time purchases structured around single-player storylines, one dramatically shaped by internet networking and instantaneous payments infrastructure software.
  But shifting gamers farther away from one-off purchases wasn’t even the gaming industry’s most fundamental reconsideration of the year, a space reserved for a coordinated move by the world’s richest companies to upend the console wars with an invisible competitor. It’s perhaps unsurprising that the most full-featured plays in this arena are coming from the cloud services triumvirate, with Google, Microsoft and Amazon each making significant strides in recent months.
 The driving force for this change is both the maturation of virtual desktop streaming and continued developer movement toward online cross-play between gaming platforms, a trend long resisted by legacy platform owners intent on maintaining siloed network effects that pushed gamers toward buying the same consoles that their friends owned.
 The cross-play trend reached a fever pitch in recent years as entities like  Epic Games’   Fortnite developed massive user bases that gave developers exceptional influence over the deals they struck with platform owners.
 While a trend toward deeper cross-play planted the seeds for new corporate players in the gaming world, it has been the tech companies with the deepest pockets that have pioneered the most concerted plays to side-load a third-party candidate into the console wars.
 It’s already clear to plenty of gamers that even in their nascent stages, cloud-gaming platforms aren’t meeting up to their hype and standalone efforts aren’t technologically stunning enough to make up for the apparent lack of selection in the content libraries.

note:
多年以来，复古游戏主要潜伏在游戏界肮脏的腹部。尽管一些致力于经典游戏的拥护者寻求原始硬件，但大多数玩家却没有。毕竟，在PC上，被黑的手持设备或智能手机上启动视频游戏ROM或磁盘映像要比与日益稀有的，容易出故障的游戏机和旧计算机作斗争要容易得多。
 曾经尝试过官方的复古产品，但2016年的NES Mini（或使用其全称，Nintendo Classic Mini：Nintendo娱乐系统）是第一个充分捕捉公众想象力的产品。有了这个小巧的控制台，您就不再可以在非游戏设计的系统上玩无权的游戏。
  相反，任天堂通过熟悉的控制器和官方许可的游戏来提供真实性，这些游戏装在塑料壳中，这是实际NES的吐痰-尽管看起来像是在洗净。
 任天堂小巧的小工具的成功导致了一系列的模仿者，从小型的Commodore 64到小型的Sony PlayStation。有些很好；许多人有缺陷，比赛经验只是表面上的。幸运的是，一些公司希望走得更远，而不是制造另一个微型即插即用电视控制台。
  ZX Spectrum Next是一台来自另一宇宙的机器，在该机器中，辛克莱从未被出售给Amstrad，而是建造了一台计算机，以承接Amiga和Atari ST的力量。
    另外两家公司又回到了游戏的过去，并给自己设定了同样雄心勃勃的挑战：重建令人兴奋，嘈杂，在视觉上引人注目的经典机壳，而这些经典机壳曾经在商场中找到过。 “我不仅将它们视为游戏，还拥有独特的形状，艺术，声音和灯光，相互吸引，从口袋中赚钱。” Quarter Arcades创始人Numskull Designs的管理合伙人Matt Precious解释道。 “令我失望的是，当LP之类的实体物品在数字下载日益无菌的世界中蓬勃发展时，您无法购买这些机器的型号。”
 Quarter Arcades随后诞生为一个“试图捕捉四分之一游戏柜历史的游戏”项目。机器尺寸精确，包括控制装置，并播放原始的街机ROM。但是仔细观察，就会发现细节过于逼真：控制面板图片的粗糙纹理；通过精心布置扬声器，模仿原始驾驶室的声学效果；重新创建“太空侵略者”的“幻影”幻影效果，使图形“漂浮”在照明的背景下-通过拆除和逆向工程原始驾驶室来实现。 
New Wave Toys的创始人Shilo Prychak说：“我们是拱廊历史专业的学生，​​我们正在努力捕捉我们产品中原始机器的灵魂。” New Wave的RepliCade产品采用与Numskull相似的方法，但比例更小：1：6。
  “真实性是我们的首要任务，我们通过从原始机器中捕获尽可能多的细节层来实现这一目标，” Prychak补充道。他不是在开玩笑-最新的RepliCade，《龙的巢穴》基于标志性的1983 LaserDisc游戏，该游戏以迪士尼风格的动画代替了光栅或矢量图形。适当地，它不仅包括原始游戏的橱柜装饰，控制布局和辅助记分牌，还包括后置储物抽屉中的复制品LaserDisc播放器，放置在其内部的复制品LaserDisc和不可能的小复制品遥控器。
  这些细节可以说是外围的，纯粹是装饰性的，但是它们确实展示了这些出租车如何尽可能地捕捉原始体验，而不仅仅是回声。正如Numskull Designs的创意总监Karl Mizen所说的那样，这些橱柜不能“只是玩复古游戏”，因为许多流行的老式游戏在各种应用商店和在线市场上都可以买到，以吸引和证明它们的存在，他们必须做更多的事情。
   就是说，如果不能玩游戏，这些产品将成为装饰品。因此，需要付出大量的努力来确保游戏体验扎实。 Mizen说，人们误以为“四分之一街机店”的名字排在第一位，其他一切都紧随其后。但是1：4比例对他的团队来说是一个不错的选择，在美学和可玩性之间取得了平衡：艺术品的大小足以让您欣赏所有细节。而且，操纵杆和按钮足够大，可以舒适地操作，我们的5英寸屏幕可清晰显示游戏画面。”他说，如果橱柜无法按此尺寸播放，它们会变大，以保持所有尺寸。
 RepliCade装置以1：6比例缩放时，其控件具有更大的自由度-例如，Tempest的旋转刻度盘要比严格精确的旋转刻度盘大得多。但是该公司投入了大量时间来优化各种控制方式，包括Centipede的微型轨迹球，Street Fighter II的微动操纵杆和按钮，以及即将到来的1942年的片簧式操纵杆。
  Dragon’s Lair也标志着不再关注更广泛的游戏环境。 “我们从Kickstarter广告系列中了解到，社区主要是希望使用板载控件和内置屏幕来玩游戏，但喜欢其他选择，” Prychak说。 “因此，我们增加了USB控制器支持和HDMI连接。我们会在有意义的情况下为即将推出的游戏添加类似的功能，以最大限度地发挥功能并为收藏家创造更多价值。”
   纯粹主义者可能会担心副本机柜后部的非真实USB和HDMI端口，但是重新创建旧游戏却很平衡。关于真实性的任何妥协都有助于改善玩家的状况，这对Prychak才是真正重要的。 “对于人来说，没有比玩具有原始经验的原始机器更重要的事情了，＆＃34;他说。 ＆＃34;因此，我们将继续开发我们的产品，以带回这些共鸣，并创建其他配件，例如我们的可缩放背光的拱廊地毯和为驾驶室提供USB电源的复制机。” 
Mizen说，最好的复古游戏不仅可以专注于过去，还可以为未来提供信息。 “整代游戏玩家都错过了这个电子游戏时代，＆＃34; 他说。 ＆＃34;通过忠实地重建街机游戏柜，我们想认为我们已经完整保存了这些出色的游戏，供大家体验。”  💡2020年惨淡，但是这32位创新者正在为我们所有人打造更美好的未来  with挑选最好的越野跑鞋走出城市  🔊收听每个星期五播报的科学，技术和文化周《有线》播客  2021年《有线世界》是有关将影响来年的思想，趋势，技术，人员和公司的必不可少的简报。  立即购买
note_en:
For years, retro gaming primarily lurked in the game world’s seedy underbelly. Although some dedicated advocates of classic titles sought out original hardware, most players did not. After all, it was far easier to launch a videogame ROM or disk image on a PC, hacked handheld or smartphone than battle with increasingly rare, failure-prone consoles and computers of old.
 There had been attempts at official retro products, but 2016’s NES Mini (or to use its full name, the Nintendo Classic Mini: Nintendo Entertainment System) was the first to fully capture the public’s imagination. With this tiny console, you no longer played games you had no rights to, on systems they weren’t designed for.
  Nintendo instead offered authenticity by way of familiar controllers and officially licensed games housed inside a plastic shell that was the spit of an actual NES — albeit one that looked like it had gone through a hot wash.
 The success of Nintendo’s diminutive gadget led to a flurry of copycats, from a tiny Commodore 64 to a miniaturised Sony PlayStation. Some were good; many were flawed, with the play experience only being surface deep. Fortunately, some companies wanted to go further than fashioning yet another miniature plug-and-play TV console.
  One, the ZX Spectrum Next, brought into being a machine from an alternate universe in which Sinclair was never sold to Amstrad and instead built a computer to take on the might of the Amiga and Atari ST.
    Two other companies headed further back into gaming’s past and set themselves an equally ambitious challenge: recreating the exciting, noisy, visually arresting classic cabinets you once found in arcades. “I always saw them as more than just a game, with their unique shapes, art, sounds and lights acting together to lure money from your pocket,” explains Matt Precious, managing partner at Quarter Arcades creators Numskull Designs. “I was disappointed you couldn’t purchase models of these machines during a time when physical items like LPs were booming in an increasingly sterile world of digital downloads.”
 Quarter Arcades was subsequently born as a project “trying to capture a piece of gaming history” in quarter-scale cabinets. The machines are in exact scale, including the controls, and play the original arcade ROMs. But look closer and there’s an obsessive level of detail: the rough texture of the control panel art; mimicking an original cab’s acoustics by careful speaker positioning; recreating the  Space Invaders ‘Pepper’s ghost’ illusion effect where graphics ‘float’ above an illuminated backdrop — all realised by dismantling and reverse-engineering original cabs.
 “We are arcade history students, trying our best to capture the original machine’s soul in our products,” says New Wave Toys founder Shilo Prychak. New Wave’s RepliCade products follow a similar approach to Numskull’s, but at an even tinier 1:6 scale.
  “Authenticity is our first priority and we achieve this by capturing as many layers of detail from the original machines as possible,” Prychak adds. He’s not kidding — the most recent RepliCade,  Dragon’s Lair, is based on the iconic 1983 LaserDisc game, which featured Disney-style animation in place of raster or vector graphics. Suitably, it not only includes the original game’s cabinet art, control layout and secondary scoreboards, but also a replica LaserDisc player within a rear storage drawer, a replica LaserDisc to put inside it and an impossibly small replica remote.
  These details are arguably peripheral and purely cosmetic, but they do showcase how these cabs are about capturing as much of the original experience as possible, rather than being a mere echo. As Numskull Designs creative director Karl Mizen puts it, these cabinets cannot “just be about playing retro games,” given that many popular old-school titles are readily available on a range of app stores and online marketplaces — to appeal and justify their existence, they must do more.
   That said, without being able to play the games, these products would be ornaments. Plenty of effort therefore goes into making sure the play experience is solid. Mizen says there’s a misconception that the Quarter Arcades name came first and everything else followed; but the 1:4 scale was for his team a sweet spot, balancing aesthetics and playability: “The machines look impressive on display. The artwork’s big enough for you to appreciate all the details. But also, the joysticks and buttons are large enough to handle comfortably, and our 5in screens provide a clear view of the games.” He says that had the cabinets not been playable at this size, they would have grown, to keep everything in scale.
 At 1:6 scale, RepliCade units take more liberties with their controls — for example,  Tempest’s rotary dial is much larger than it would be if being strictly accurate. But the company has invested a lot of time in optimising a much more varied range of controls, including  Centipede’s tiny trackball,  Street Fighter II’s microswitch joystick and buttons, and a leaf spring-style joystick in the upcoming  1942.
  Dragon’s Lair also marks a departure in having an eye on wider gaming contexts. “We learned from our Kickstarter campaign that the community mostly wants to play using on-board controls and the built-in screen, but appreciates other options,” says Prychak. “So we added USB controller support and HDMI connectivity. We’ll add similar features to upcoming games when it makes sense, to maximise functionality and create more value for collectors.”
   Purists might gripe about non-authentic USB and HDMI ports around the back of a replica cabinet, but recreating old games is about balance. Any compromises regarding authenticity are in service to improving things for players, which is for Prychak what really matters. “Nothing is more important to people than playing original machines with their original experiences," he says. "So we’ll continue to evolve our products to bring back those vibes and create additional accessories, like our in-scale backlight-reactive arcade carpets and a replica change machine that provides USB power to the cabs.”
 Mizen says that rather than focusing solely on the past, retro-gaming at its best can also inform the future. “A whole generation of gamers missed out on this age of videogames," he says. "By recreating arcade cabinets in a faithful way, we like to think we’ve preserved these fantastic games in their entirety, for everyone to experience.”

上周，在备受争议的情况下，Slack宣布将自己以277亿美元的高价卖给科技巨头Salesforce。从许多方面来看，这应该是一个令人难以置信的成就和成功故事。实际上，它代表了Slack的决定性面貌。Slack此前曾明确表示，尽管来自Microsoft大量模仿产品团队的新竞争，它仍希望保持独立。
   我们的自由市场交易的前提是，好的创新产品将胜过像微软这样根深蒂固的公司发布的效果较差的产品。但是Slack决定被Salesforce收购的事实表明，今天的情况恰恰相反。 Slack只是硅谷“防御性”收购中的许多故事之一，在该收购中，公司不再能够独立与科技巨头竞争。这些拥有近乎无限资金和广泛客户关系的巨人经常滥用自身优势，欺负规模较小的新贵。即使是Slack，它开发出了令人难以置信的强大产品并以臭名昭著的效率运作，也无法在与Microsoft的对抗中保持独立。而且，如果像Slack这样的公司无法忍受公司实力的巩固，那么消费者自由选择最好和最有用的产品的能力就会受到威胁。
 我永远不会忘记我在阅读2010年代筹款头条新闻时感到的震惊。当Slack作为“另一个Messenger应用程序”，其产品没有真正的方法来抵御Big Tech时，如何筹集数亿美元呢？但是使用Slack使我成为一个信徒。产品奇迹般地使工作变得有趣。在沙漠中成为Microsoft Outlook之后成为Slack用户，就像在长期干旱之后喝一杯新鲜的水一样。通过专注于用户体验并提供直观的设计，Slack帮助改变了聊天方式。我们现在在Facebook帖子，Twitter DM甚至Microsoft Teams上看到的简单的“表情符号反应”选项都可以追溯到这一具有影响力的设计变更。
  Slack创造了最好的产品，而且它是如此出色，以至在发布后仅18个月内就获得了100万用户。但是，这种增长从2016年开始就开始放缓，尽管自那以后的四年中，该公司的用户群仍然增长了三倍，但这仍代表着一个远低于创始人所说的雄心壮志的增长因素。大多数创业公司都希望看到这种增长。无论如何，这都不容易，但是经济放缓表明Slack面临很大的问题。
 在2016年，已经拥有客户群和销售团队来征服市场的Microsoft决定从本质上复制Slack并通过将其与Microsoft专业套件捆绑在一起使其免费。在Slack的用户群增加了两倍的同一四年中，Microsoft Teams声称它获得了1.15亿用户，这向世界展示了当Big Tech想要一块产品时，产品质量无法挽救一家初创公司。
 正如Matt Stoller在其时事通讯“ BIG”中指出的那样，Microsoft拥有向现有客户免费提供或免费提供其新产品，并将其与现有产品系列捆绑销售的良好记录。在具有反托拉斯法律功能的社会中，此类活动将是非法的。”这种做法使该公司在1990年代遇到了联邦反托拉斯监管机构的麻烦，微软也在这里部署了相同的手册，从而导致Slack在今年早些时候在欧盟对微软提出了反托拉斯诉讼。
 但是Slack的领导人一定已经决定，法院将无法足够迅速地采取行动，而是计划以另一种方式挽救该公司。为了跟上微软的步伐，Slack需要一个更大的企业销售团队，他们在科技巨头Salesforce中找到了这家公司，因此我确信这笔收购真是苦乐参半。 
Slack并不是第一个因相对较低的举动（例如复制界面或更改使用条款）而破坏其业务的公司。企业遭受这种垄断性权力差异的恐怖故事太多了。其中之一就是Vevo，由于来自YouTube的压力越来越大，该公司被迫在2018年关闭大部分产品。在这种情况下，更有趣的是YouTube之前曾投资Vevo，许多初创公司创始人认为，这是Big Tech在竞争对手可以克服彼此之间巨大的实力差异并取得成功的机会时“对赌”。
  当初创企业开始在一个被巨人视为对其业务构成威胁的领域获得关注时，拥有巨大资金的斗气公司将其作为目标，以阻止他们成为未来的重要竞争对手。 Instagram也许是最著名的例子。 Instagram联合创始人凯文·西斯特罗姆（Kevin Systrom）与投资者马特·科勒（Matt Cohler）之间的私下对话表明，科技巨头高管与暴发户之间的掠夺性关系。随着Instagram越来越受到用户的喜爱，所谓的扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）想要“销毁”该应用程序的威胁越来越大。昨天宣布，Facebook对Instagram的收购现在构成了数十个州和联邦监管机构进行的新反托拉斯调查的基础。
 最近几年，两党对遏制大型科技公司的压倒性表现出了极大的兴趣，甚至导致了将其拆分的呼吁。然而，尽管美国立法者在反托拉斯听证会上对感知到的反政治偏见进行了质疑，但我们的监管机构仍在继续批准大规模收购，这表明他们将无法保护我们的自由市场免受其真正威胁：巨大规模所蕴含的不可动摇的权力随着时间的推移，这些公司积累了大量的金钱，客户关系和劳动力。值得注意的是，尽管Slack的情况证明了微软在企业市场上的强大实力，但微软并未参加在国会山举行的众多反托拉斯听证会。
 在最近有许多其他收购行动的背景下，Slack的情况只说明了创始人每天生活的故事的一小部分。在与潜在投资者交流时，每个初创公司创始人现在都必须回答一个基本问题：“您将如何与科技巨头竞争？”这个问题如此普遍，对我们所有人来说应该是一个闪烁的红灯。
 确实，大型创新来自科技巨头，但它们也来自使命，专业知识和目标狭窄的小型公司。对于初创企业和消费者而言，将创新型初创企业整合到少数几家大公司手中应该是令人不安的。这意味着我们可能很快就会失去仅基于优点选择首选工具的能力。而且，除非我们希望所有数字产品都来自少数几家公司的未来，否则该是重新平衡竞争环境的时候了。
  Liz O’Sullivan是AI监控公司Arthur的联合创始人兼负责AI副总裁。她还担任STOP（监视技术监督项目）的技术总监，在该项目中，她致力于纽约政策，以遏制威胁到我们公民自由的技术，尤其是在边缘化社区中。她是“制止杀手机器人运动”的积极成员，她代表联合国在此倡导。
note_en:
Last week, and with much  fanfare, Slack announced that it would sell itself to tech behemoth Salesforce for a whopping $27.7 billion. By many measurements, this should be an incredible achievement and success story. In reality, it represents a decisive  about-face for Slack, which had previously made clear that, despite new competition from Microsoft’s largely copycat product Teams, it wanted to remain independent.
   Our free market trades on the assumption that good, innovative products will prevail over less effective ones released by entrenched firms like Microsoft. But Slack’s decision to be acquired by Salesforce indicates that today, the exact opposite is true. Slack is but one of many stories in Silicon Valley of a “defensive” acquisition, where a company is no longer able to compete independently against the tech giants. These giants, armed with nearly limitless funds and extensive client relationships, frequently abuse their advantage and bully smaller upstarts  into oblivion. Even Slack, which built an incredibly powerful product and operated with notorious efficiency, could not stay independent in a match-up against Microsoft. And if a company like Slack can’t stand up to the consolidation of corporate power, consumers’ ability to freely choose the best and most useful product is at risk.
 I’ll never forget the shock I felt reading fundraising headlines in the 2010s. How could Slack, as “just another messenger app,” raise hundreds of millions of dollars when its product had no real way to defend itself against Big Tech? But using Slack made me a believer. Miraculously, the product made work  fun. Becoming a Slack user after the desert that was Microsoft Outlook was like a fresh drink of water after a long drought. By focusing on the user experience, and offering intuitive design, Slack helped change the paradigm of chat. The simple “emoji react” options we see now on Facebook posts, Twitter DMs, and even Microsoft Teams can all trace their lineage to this impactful design change.
  Slack made the best product, and it was such a good one that it gained  a million users in a mere 18 months following launch. But this growth slowed starting in 2016, and even though the company’s user base has still tripled in size in the four years since then, this represented a growth factor that fell far short of the founders’ stated ambition. Most startups would love to see that kind of growth; it’s not easy by any means—but the slowdown revealed that Slack had a big problem on its hands.
 In 2016, Microsoft, which already had the customer base and sales force to conquer the market, decided to essentially copy Slack and make it free by bundling it with the Microsoft professional suite. In those same four years where Slack’s user base tripled, Microsoft Teams claims it  gained 115 million users, showing the world that product quality can’t save a startup when Big Tech wants a piece.
 As Matt Stoller notes in his newsletter “ BIG“, Microsoft has a track record of giving “away its new product for no or low cost to existing clients, and [bundling] it with existing product lines. In a society with functional antitrust laws, such activity would be illegal.” This practice landed the company in trouble with federal antitrust regulators in the 1990s, and Microsoft has deployed the same playbook here as well—leading to Slack’s  antitrust complaint against Microsoft in the EU earlier this year.
 But Slack’s leaders must have decided that the courts wouldn’t be able to act quickly enough, and instead planned to save the company another way. In order to  keep pace with Microsoft, Slack needed a bigger enterprise sales team, which they found in tech giant Salesforce, leading to an acquisition I’m quite sure was bittersweet.
  Slack is hardly the first company to have its business undermined by a relatively low-lift action like copying an interface or changing terms of use. There are so many horror stories of businesses suffering from this monopolistic power disparity. One of these is Vevo, which was forced to shutter most of its offering in 2018 due to  increased pressure from YouTube. Even more interesting in that case is that YouTube had previously invested in Vevo—a practice many startup founders consider to be Big Tech “hedging its bets” on the off chance a rival can overcome the vast power differentials between them and succeed.
  When startups begin to gain traction in an area the giants perceive as a threat to their business, vindictive firms with huge coffers target them to stop them becoming future competitors of significance. Instagram is perhaps the most famous example of this.  Private conversations between Instagram cofounder Kevin Systrom and investor Matt Cohler show the predatory relationship between tech giant executives and upstart rivals. As Instagram became more and more beloved by users, alleged threats of Mark Zuckerberg’s desire to “destroy” the app grew stronger. Facebook’s acquisition of Instagram now forms the basis of a new antitrust inquiry from dozens of states and federal regulators,  announced yesterday.
 The last few years have shown a remarkable bipartisan interest in curbing the overwhelming power of big tech companies, even leading to calls to break them up. However, while American lawmakers quibble about perceived  anti-political bias during antitrust hearings, our regulators continue to approve huge  acquisitions, showing that they won’t protect our free market from its true threat: an untouchable degree of power that is enshrined in the massive amounts of money, customer relationships, and workforce labor these companies have amassed over time. Notably, despite how Slack’s situation demonstrates Microsoft’s unchecked power in the enterprise market, Microsoft has not been part of the many antitrust hearings happening on Capitol Hill.
 Slack’s situation, amid a backdrop of many other  recent acquisitions, tells but one tiny fraction of a story that founders live through every day. When speaking to potential investors, every startup founder now has to answer a fundamental question: “How will you compete with the tech giants?” That this question is so commonplace should be a flashing red light to us all.
 It’s true that big innovations come from tech behemoths, but they also come from  smaller companies with narrow missions, expertise, and goals. For startups and consumers both, the consolidation of innovative startups into the hands of just a few large firms should be troubling. It means we could soon lose the ability to choose our preferred tools based on merit alone. And unless we want a future where all digital products come from just a few companies, it’s time to rebalance the playing field.
  Liz O’Sullivan is a cofounder and VP of Responsible AI at  Arthur, the AI monitoring company. She also serves as Technology Director for  STOP (The Surveillance Technology Oversight Project), where she works on New York policy to curb the technology that threatens our civil liberties, especially among marginalized communities. She is an active member of the Campaign to Stop Killer Robots, where she advocates on their behalf at the UN.
title: Slack数十亿美元出售给Salesforce背后的黑暗现实
title_en: The dark reality behind Slack’s billion-dollar sale to Salesforce

    为了计算函数f，CPython必须计算表达式x +7。我想问的问题是：CPython如何做到这一点？您可能会想到诸如__add __（）和__radd __（）之类的特殊方法。当我们在类上定义这些方法时，可以使用+运算符添加该类的实例。因此，您可能会认为CPython会执行以下操作：

 艰难的现实要复杂得多。实际发生的情况取决于x是多少。例如，如果x是用户定义类的实例，则上述算法类似于事实。但是，如果x是内置类型的实例（例如int或float），则CPython根本不会调用任何特殊方法。
   让我们将此算法应用于函数f。编译器将此函数的主体转换为以下字节码： 
$ python -m dis f.py ... 2 0 LOAD_FAST 0（x）2 LOAD_CONST 1（7）4 BINARY_ADD 6 RETURN_VALUE
  BINARY_ADD从堆栈中弹出两个值，将它们相加并将结果推回堆栈中。
 VM如何将两个值相加？要回答这个问题，我们需要了解这些值是什么。对我们来说，7是int的实例，而x是任何东西。但是，对于VM，一切都是Python对象。 VM推送到堆栈并从堆栈弹出的所有值都是指向PyObject结构的指针（因此，短语＆＃34; Python中的所有内容都是对象）。
 VM不需要知道如何添加整数或字符串，即如何进行算术或连接序列。它需要知道的是每个Python对象都有一个类型。反过来，类型知道有关其对象的所有信息。例如，int类型知道如何添加整数，而float类型则知道如何添加浮点数。因此，VM要求类型执行操作。
 这种简化的说明抓住了解决方案的本质，但同时也省略了许多重要的细节。为了获得更真实的画面，我们需要了解什么是真正的Python对象和类型以及它们如何工作。
  我们已经在第3部分中讨论了Python对象。在这里值得重复讨论。
  typedef struct _object {_PyObject_HEAD_EXTRA //宏，仅用于调试目的Py_ssize_t ob_refcnt; PyTypeObject * ob_type; } PyObject; 
我们说过，VM将任何Python对象都视为PyObject。那怎么可能？ C编程语言没有类和继承的概念。不过，可以在C中实现可以称为单一继承的东西。 C标准指出，指向任何结构的指针都可以转换为指向其第一个成员的指针，反之亦然。因此，我们可以＆＃34;扩展＆＃34;通过定义第一个成员为PyObject的新结构来实现PyObject。
   浮点对象存储PyObject存储的所有内容以及浮点值ob_fval。 C标准仅声明我们可以将指向PyFloatObject的指针转换为指向PyObject的指针，反之亦然：
  VM之所以将每个Python对象都视为PyObject，是因为它需要访问的只是对象的类型。类型也是Python对象，是PyTypeObject结构的实例：
 // PyTypeObject是＆＃34; struct _typeobject＆＃34;的typedef struct _typeobject {PyVarObject ob_base; //扩展PyObject_VAR_HEAD宏const char * tp_name; / *对于打印，格式为＆＃34;＆lt; module＆gt;。＆lt; name＆gt;＆＃34; * / Py_ssize_t tp_basicsize，tp_itemsize; / *对于分配* / / *实现标准操作的方法* /析构函数tp_dealloc; Py_ssize_t tp_vectorcall_offset; getattrfunc tp_getattr; setattrfunc tp_setattr; PyAsyncMethods * tp_as_async; / *以前称为tp_compare（Python 2）或tp_reserved（Python 3）* / reprfunc tp_repr; / *标准类的方法套件* / PyNumberMethods * tp_as_number; PySequenceMethods * tp_as_sequence; PyMappingMethods * tp_as_mapping; / *更多标准操作（此处为二进制兼容性）* / hashfunc tp_hash; ternaryfunc tp_call; reprfunc tp_str; getattrofunc tp_getattro; setattrofunc tp_setattro; / *访问对象作为输入/输出缓冲区的功能* / PyBufferProcs * tp_as_buffer; / *标志定义可选/扩展功能的存在* /无符号长tp_flags; const char * tp_doc; / *文档字符串* / / *在2.0版中分配的含义* / / *所有可访问对象的调用函数* / traverseproc tp_traverse; / *删除对包含对象的引用* /查询tp_clear; / *在2.1版中分配的含义* / / *丰富的比较* / richcmpfunc tp_richcompare; / *弱参考启用码* / Py_ssize_t tp_weaklistoffset; / *迭代器* / getiterfunc tp_iter; iternextfunc tp_iternext; / *属性描述符和子类化的东西* / struct PyMethodDef * tp_methods; struct PyMemberDef * tp_members; struct PyGetSetDef * tp_getset; struct _typeobject * tp_base; PyObject * tp_dict; descrgetfunc tp_descr_get; descrsetfunc tp_descr_set; Py_ssize_t tp_dictoffset; initproc tp_init; allocfunc tp_alloc; newfunc tp_new; freefunc tp_free; / *低级自由内存例程* /查询tp_is_gc; / *对于PyObject_IS_GC * / PyObject * tp_bases; PyObject * tp_mro; / *方法解析顺序* / PyObject * tp_cache; PyObject * tp_subclasses; PyObject * tp_weaklist;析构函数tp_del; / *类型属性高速缓存版本标记。在2.6版中添加* / unsigned int tp_version_tag;析构函数tp_finalize; vectorcallfunc tp_vectorcall; };

 但是，由于PyVarObject的第一个成员是PyObject，所以仍然可以将指向类型的指针转​​换为指向PyObject的指针。 
那么，什么是类型？为什么它有这么多成员？类型确定该类型的对象的行为。类型的每个成员（称为插槽）负责对象行为的特定方面。例如：
 tp_str是指向为该类型的对象实现str（）的函数的指针。
 tp_hash是指向为该类型的对象实现hash（）的函数的指针。
 一些插槽（称为子插槽）在套件中分组在一起。套件只是包含相关插槽的结构。例如，PySequenceMethods结构是实现序列协议的一组子插槽：

 由于我们对CPython如何添加对象感兴趣，因此我们找到负责添加的插槽。必须至少有一个这样的插槽。仔细检查PyTypeObject结构后，我们发现它具有＆＃34; number＆＃34;套件PyNumberMethods，该套件的第一个插槽是一个名为nd_add的二进制函数：

 看来nb_add插槽正是我们要寻找的。关于此广告位自然会引起两个问题：
